question:
Which of the following statements are related to Liberalism?
A. It is an ideology of the industrialised West
B. It advocated the virtues of laissez-faire
C. It reflects the aspirations of rising industrial/commercial classes
D. Liberalism and Communism are closely linked
E. Locke was a key thinker of liberalism

Choose the correct answer:
options: A, C, D and E only
B, C, D and E only
A, B, C and E only
D, E, A and B only
explanation: A, B, C and E are true of liberalism. Statement D is false — liberalism and communism are ideologically opposed, not closely linked.

question: The 'Uniform Civil Code' is a part of which section of the Indian Constitution?
explanation: The Uniform Civil Code is contained in Article 44, which falls under the Directive Principles of State Policy (Part IV) — and is therefore not enforceable by courts.

topic: Making of the Constitution & Constituent Assembly
question:
Match List-I (Book) with List-II (Author):

List-I
A. The Framing of India's Constitution: A Study
B. The Indian Constitution: Cornerstone of a Nation
C. The Transfer of Power in India
D. Kashmir: A Study in India-Pakistan Relations

List-II
I. V.P. Menon
II. Sisir K. Gupta
III. B. Shiva Rao
IV. Granville Austin
explanation: B. Shiva Rao wrote 'The Framing of India's Constitution'; Granville Austin wrote 'The Indian Constitution: Cornerstone of a Nation'; V.P. Menon wrote 'The Transfer of Power in India'; Sisir K. Gupta wrote 'Kashmir: A Study in India-Pakistan Relations'. So A-III, B-IV, C-I, D-II.

question:
Match List-I (International Event) with List-II (Year):

List-I
A. Gorbachev became Soviet leader
B. Soviet invasion of Afghanistan
C. Cuban Missile Crisis
D. Soviet invasion of Hungary

List-II
I. 1962
II. 1956
III. 1985
IV. 1979
explanation: Gorbachev became Soviet leader in 1985; the Soviet invasion of Afghanistan was in 1979; the Cuban Missile Crisis in 1962; and the Soviet invasion of Hungary in 1956. So A-III, B-IV, C-I, D-II.

question:
Match List-I (Former Chief Minister) with List-II (State):

List-I
A. Karpoori Thakur
B. Ram Naresh Yadav
C. Ravi Shankar Shukla
D. Prem Kumar Dhumal

List-II
I. Himachal Pradesh
II. Madhya Pradesh
III. Bihar
IV. Uttar Pradesh
explanation: Karpoori Thakur was CM of Bihar; Ram Naresh Yadav of Uttar Pradesh; Ravi Shankar Shukla was the first CM of Madhya Pradesh; Prem Kumar Dhumal of Himachal Pradesh. So A-III, B-IV, C-II, D-I.

question:
Match List-I (Theory) with List-II (Propounder):

List-I
A. Scientific Management
B. Linking Pin Model
C. Alienation of Bureaucracy
D. New Public Administration

List-II
I. Karl Marx
II. Frederick W. Taylor
III. Dwight Waldo
IV. Rensis Likert
explanation: Scientific Management – F.W. Taylor; the Linking Pin Model – Rensis Likert; alienation (and the critique of bureaucracy) – Karl Marx; New Public Administration – associated with Dwight Waldo (Minnowbrook). So A-II, B-IV, C-I, D-III.

question: The National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (NREGA) came into force / was implemented in the year:
explanation: NREGA was enacted in 2005 and came into force in February 2006 (later renamed MGNREGA in 2009).
